question: Top 10 customers by total rental revenue with their city and country
schema_context:
-- public.payment_p2022_07
CREATE TABLE public.payment_p2022_07 (
  payment_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  customer_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  staff_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  rental_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  amount NUMERIC(5, 2) NOT NULL,
  payment_date TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);

annotation.enriched_question: Top 10 customers by total rental revenue with their city and country from payment, payment_p2022_01, payment_p2022_03, payment_p2022_05, payment_p2022_07, customer, and address tables

generated_sql:
WITH payments AS (
    SELECT customer_id, amount
    FROM public.payment
    UNION ALL
    SELECT customer_id, amount
    FROM public.payment_p2022_01
    UNION ALL
    SELECT customer_id, amount
    FROM public.payment_p2022_03
    UNION ALL
    SELECT customer_id, amount
    FROM public.payment_p2022_05
    UNION ALL
    SELECT customer_id, amount
    FROM public.payment_p2022_07
),
customer_revenue AS (
    SELECT p.customer_id, SUM(p.amount) AS total_revenue
    FROM payments p
    GROUP BY p.customer_id
),
customer_info AS (
    SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, a.city, a.country
    FROM public.customer c
    JOIN public.address a ON c.address_id = a.address_id
)
SELECT ci.first_name, ci.last_name, a.city, a.country, cr.total_revenue,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY cr.total_revenue DESC) AS rank
FROM customer_revenue cr
JOIN customer_info ci ON cr.customer_id = ci.customer_id
JOIN public.address a ON ci.address_id = a.address_id
ORDER BY cr.total_revenue DESC;
